Identity.History: Pericles was a leading Athenian statesman, general, and orator of the 5th century BC, guiding Athens during much of its Golden Age. He strengthened democracy, expanded naval power, and directed the rebuilding of the Acropolis, including the Parthenon. Under his leadership, Athens became the cultural and political center of the Greek world, though his imperial policies helped intensify rivalry with Sparta. Pericles was closely associated with Aspasia of Miletus, his companion and intellectual partner, who was famous for her learning and influence. He died during the plague of Athens in 429 BC.
